channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Terraform guide to IAM Policies: The Two Cases You Need to Know
item.description: When to attach a ready made policy and when to write your own When you first start writing IAM setup in Terraform, the examples online can feel like they contradict each other. One tutorial writes...

item.title: Creating an internet for AI, or shall we?
item.description: This is more of a rant than an article but I feel like we should start talking about this now rather than later. Till some time ago, my most visited places were Twitter and DevTo. (They were so...

item.title: How to Selectively Route OpenVPN Traffic Using PassWall2 on OpenWRT
item.description: If you run OpenVPN directly on your OpenWRT router, it usually hijacks your entire internet connection. But what if you only want specific apps, websites, or devices to use the VPN? By combining...

item.title: Why I Built OSE Auditor
item.description: A few months ago, I ran into a problem I couldn't ignore. I was building software with payments, wallets, settlements and complex workflows. Everything looked fine. The tests passed. The API...

item.title: I Benchmarked DeepSeek, Qwen, Kimi & GLM for 30 Days — The Numbers
item.description: I Benchmarked DeepSeek, Qwen, Kimi & GLM for 30 Days — The Numbers I'll be honest — I didn't set out to write this. I set out to pick one Chinese LLM family for a client project and move on with...

item.title: Optimizing Mobile Web Apps for Low-Bandwidth Networks
item.description: Mobile web performance isn't just about fast desktop speeds throttled down; it’s an entirely different environment. When developers assume pristine 5G environments, users in high-latency or remote...

item.title: Range anxiety
item.description: The storm developed quickly over west central Illinois on April 17th, first as a single high-intensity system called a supercell, and then later that evening transforming into a long squall line...

item.title: Building with mini, Part 4/9: The main loop — next plan do done
item.description: pycalc has a vision (Part 2) and a backlog (Part 3) — and still not a single line of Python. Today we fix that. Four commands form the heart of mini: propose, break down, build, close. One turn of...

item.title: Fake Praise, Real Money
item.description: Patrick Radden Keefe, the best-selling author of Say Nothing and Empire of Pain, both adapted into critically acclaimed television series, has become accustomed to a daily ritual that has nothing...

item.title: Como rodar Classic ASP no Linux: Um guia passo a passo para instalar o AxonASP no Ubuntu ou Debian
item.description: Introdução Hospedar aplicações Classic ASP (Active Server Pages) tradicionalmente significava estar acorrentado ao Windows Server e ao IIS. Para desenvolvedores que dão manutenção em aplicações...

item.title: The cube was a lie: up close with our Dbrand Companion Cube before it gets destroyed
item.description: Dbrand is the company that shipped a product that told Nintendo's legal team to "go fuck yourself," and the company that had a second set of lawyer-dodging PS5 plates ready when Sony threatened to...

item.title: The Step-by-Step Process of Penetration Testing: A Defensive Guide
item.description: The Step-by-Step Process of Penetration Testing: A Defensive Guide Learn the structured process of penetration testing to identify vulnerabilities before attackers do. This defensive cybersecurity...

item.title: Papa Johns Surveillance-Based Advertising
item.description: Papa Johns is spying on people’s buying activities to predict when they are low on food: The pizza chain recently tapped NBCUniversal, Instacart and the dentsu-owned media agency Carat for help...

item.title: Microsoft Accelerates Post-Quantum Cryptography Shift to 2029
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday said it's accelerating its quantum safe security roadmap, stating technology advances in quantum computing are making it essential to replace existing encryption standards...

item.title: Fujifilm launches two new QuickSnap cameras because Gen Z can’t get enough
item.description: Fujifilm is expanding its QuickSnap lineup with a new disposable camera focused on taking monochrome photos and another built to survive harsh outdoor environments. The $22.90 QuickSnap Black and...

item.title: Claude Helped a Hacker Find a Way to Issue Tickets to Almost Every US Music Festival
item.description: A researcher found that using Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.7, he could break into the website of Front Gate—used by every festival from Lollapalooza to Bonnaroo—and freely issue any ticket he chose.

item.title: Cleared by the US, derailed by the UK: Getty’s Shutterstock merger falls apart
item.description: Getty is planning to axe its $3.7 billion merger agreement with Shutterstock after a UK regulator imposed restrictions that would prevent part of Shutterstock's business from being included in the...

item.title: Phantom Squatting Uses AI-Hallucinated Domains for Phishing and Malware
item.description: Large language models keep inventing web addresses that do not exist. Attackers have started buying those made-up domains before anyone else can, then hosting phishing pages on them to catch...

item.title: Anthropic Restores Claude Fable 5 After U.S. Lifts Jailbreak-Linked Export Controls
item.description: Anthropic is putting Claude Fable 5 back online worldwide. On June 30, the U.S. Commerce Department lifted the export controls it had imposed on Fable and its more tightly controlled sibling...

item.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "massive, ongoing, automated password spray attack" aimed at Microsoft's Azure command-line interface (CLI), compromising dozens of accounts in the...

item.title: Researcher Analyzes 3,000 Live ClickFix Payloads, Exposing API-Driven Malware Delivery
item.description: ClickFix, the trick that fools people into running malware by hand, has quietly grown a back office. New research shows the malicious commands behind its fake "prove you're human" pages are now...

item.title: Citrix Patches Six NetScaler Flaws Allowing File Read and Denial-of-Service
item.description: Citrix on Tuesday released security updates to address multiple flaws in NetScaler ADC (formerly Citrix ADC) and NetScaler Gateway (formerly Citrix Gateway) that could be exploited by an attacker...

item.title: The ‘Father of the Internet’ is finally retiring
item.description: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.

item.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
item.description: The Trump administration's erratic approach to AI policymaking has left companies across the industry with little clarity about what will govern future model releases.

item.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
item.description: Wayve’s offering is part of a growing trend of AI startups using employee tenders as a strategic tool to attract and retain talent.

item.title: Meta is adding ridiculous ‘rate limits’ and a soft paywall to its smart glasses
item.description: Would you pay $20 a month for access to AI hardware you already own? That appears to be one of Meta's next bets. This week, it quietly announced that your glasses' Conversation Focus feature will...

item.title: Anthropic’s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
item.description: After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring...

item.title: The Trump Administration Is Lifting Its Export Controls on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable AI Models
item.description: The White House is easing restrictions on Anthropic’s most advanced AI models weeks after ordering the company to suspend access for foreign nationals.

item.title: Dish files for bankruptcy, but is not shutting down
item.description: Dish, the company that operates Dish TV and Sling TV, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy," as reported earlier by Reuters. The plan will allow the EchoStar-owned company to continue to wind down...

item.title: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
item.description: The free open source agentic program is finally invading your phone.
